perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party shall return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party may retain such information at its discretion for archival purposes only, even after the termination of this agreement. The retention of such information shall not constitute a breach of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original section requires the Recipient Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request. The modified section allows the Recipient Party to retain such information at their discretion for archival purposes, which directly contradicts the original return/destroy requirement. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Recipient Party is now granted the option to retain, despite the initial obligation to return or destroy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party may retain such information at its discretion for archival purposes only, even after the termination of this agreement. The retention of such information shall not constitute a breach of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Recipient Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon the Disclosing Party's request. However, the changed text allows the Recipient Party to retain the same information for archival purposes, even after the agreement's termination. This directly contradicts the original obligation to return or destroy, leading to an in-text contradiction, as both texts are in conflict with one another.

perturbation.original_text: If either Party breaches its promises made in this Agreement, the non-breaching Party will have available to it all remedies available under law.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Party breaches its promises made in this Agreement, the non-breaching Party will have available to it only injunctive relief. Monetary damages are explicitly waived by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original section states that all remedies available under law are available if a breach occurs. The modified section limits remedies to only injunctive relief, explicitly waiving monetary damages. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original section allows for a broader range of remedies, while the modified section limits this drastically to a specific remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party breaches its promises made in this Agreement, the non-breaching Party will have available to it only injunctive relief. Monetary damages are explicitly waived by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all remedies available under law are available if a breach occurs. The modified section limits remedies to only injunctive relief, explicitly waiving monetary damages. The contradicted text restates the modified text and confirms the limitation of remedies to injunctive relief. Thus, the change does create a contradiction as the original text allows for a broader range of remedies while the modified and contradicted sections limit this drastically.
